name: Teucer
description: Teucer, also known as Teucrus, was a legendary archer and warrior in Greek mythology, renowned for his skill with the bow. He was the son of Telamon, king of Salamis, and Hesione, daughter of King Laomedon of Troy. Teucer was a half-brother of Ajax the Great, with whom he fought valiantly in the Trojan War. While Ajax was known for his immense strength and close-quarters combat, Teucer excelled as an archer, picking off Trojan warriors from afar. He played a crucial role in the Achaean efforts to breach the walls of Troy, though his arrows were not always successful, as he was sometimes foiled by the gods. A notable instance of this was when Apollo deflected his arrows aimed at Hector. Despite his prowess, Teucer was often overshadowed by other heroes like Achilles and Ajax. After Ajax's tragic suicide, Teucer was blamed for not protecting his brother and faced exile from Salamis by his father, Telamon. Disheartened but not defeated, Teucer sailed west and founded a new city, which he named Salamis, in Cyprus, honoring his homeland. He married Eune, the daughter of King Cinyras of Cyprus, and established a new kingdom, continuing his legacy as a legendary hero. Teucer's story highlights the complexities of heroism, showcasing both his exceptional skills and the personal tragedies he endured. His exile and subsequent founding of a new Salamis demonstrate resilience and the enduring human spirit in the face of adversity, solidifying his place within Greek mythology.
subtitle: The Archer of Salamis

keyMyths.title: Teucer's Archery in the Trojan War
keyMyths.description: Teucer was a skilled archer who fought bravely in the Trojan War. He aimed arrows at Hector, but Apollo intervened, deflecting them.

keyMyths.title: Exile from Salamis
keyMyths.description: After Ajax's death, Teucer was blamed by his father, Telamon, for not protecting his brother and was exiled from Salamis. This led him to seek a new home.

keyMyths.title: Founding of Salamis in Cyprus
keyMyths.description: Teucer sailed west after his exile and founded a new city in Cyprus, which he named Salamis, honoring his original homeland. He established a new kingdom there.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Provides accounts of Teucer's role in the Trojan War.
sources.text: Provides accounts of Teucer's role in the Trojan War.

sources.author: Sophocles
sources.description: Details the aftermath of Ajax's death and Teucer's exile.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: Summarizes the key events in Teucer's life, including his founding of Salamis in Cyprus.
